summary: Backend-only testing of the new Calendar Picker endpoints (GET /api/calendar/list/{provider}, POST /api/calendar/set_default/{provider}) and updated GET /api/calendar/status. All 11 new tests PASSED. Full regression of 31 prior tests also PASSED. Total: 42/42 (100%). Verified: (1) /calendar/status now includes default_calendar_id and default_calendar_name fields for both google and microsoft (all null for unconnected demo user); (2) /calendar/list/microsoft and /calendar/list/google return HTTP 400 with detail '<provider> not connected' when no token doc exists; (3) /calendar/list/invalid_provider returns HTTP 400 'Invalid provider'; (4) /calendar/set_default/microsoft returns HTTP 400 'microsoft not connected'; (5) /calendar/set_default/invalid returns HTTP 400 'Invalid provider'; (6) /calendar/set_default/microsoft with missing calendar_id returns HTTP 422 (Pydantic validation); (7) calendar_name optional field accepted; (8) both new endpoints require auth (HTTPBearer returns 403 without header). Regression confirmed for login, bills CRUD (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/toggle_paid), bank endpoints, /calendar/status, /calendar/disconnect/microsoft, /calendar/sync_all, and OAuth start endpoints for both providers.
critical_code_review_comments: set_default_calendar: when payload.calendar_name is omitted on a subsequent change, the previously stored default_calendar_name is NOT cleared (only set when provided). This may leave a stale name attached to a new calendar_id if frontend ever sends id-only. Consider always upserting both fields (set name to None when not provided) or making frontend always send name.
set_default_calendar 'unchanged' early-return (old_cal_id == new_cal_id) does NOT update default_calendar_name even if it changed. Minor edge case for users renaming calendars on the provider side.
list_external_calendars filters Microsoft calendars by canEdit (defaulting True when missing). Graph API actually returns 'canEdit' reliably; the default True is permissive but acceptable.

set_default deletes events from OLD calendar via _ms_delete_event/_google_delete_event which use the CURRENT default_calendar_id (still the OLD one at this point since update happens after). This is correct behaviour. Verified logic flow is right.
server.py is now ~1140 lines; would benefit from splitting calendar/oauth into its own module.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing demo user (demo@billcal.app). Autouse fixture defensively disconnects both providers before tests to guarantee 'not connected' state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new calendar picker endpoints verified against public URL https://bill-reminder-hub-6.preview.emergentagent.com/api with demo user (not connected to any provider). Tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_calendar_picker.py. The 'connected' code paths (listing real calendars, moving events between calendars) could NOT be tested without a real Microsoft account consent flow; only the unconnected/validation paths were exercised. To test the connected paths (200 response from /calendar/list with real calendar list, event migration on /set_default), a real MS OAuth callback must be completed first and then tests should be authored to mock or use a live connection.
